metadata.title: US Payer & Insurer Coverage Requirements
sources: Published formulary guidelines from major US insurers
Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy (AMCP) Dossier Requirements

national_coverage_determination.description: CMS evaluates whether an item or service is reasonable and necessary for Medicare beneficiaries nationwide
key_criteria: Sufficient clinical evidence of safety and efficacy
Comparison to existing standard of care
Patient-relevant health outcomes (not just surrogate endpoints)
Adequate representation of Medicare-age population in trials
Cost-effectiveness is NOT a formal criterion but influences decisions
common_denial_reasons: Insufficient evidence of clinical benefit vs existing treatments
Surrogate endpoints without demonstrated correlation to clinical outcomes
Lack of data in Medicare-eligible population (age ≥65)
No evidence of improved health outcomes vs standard of care

local_coverage_determination.description: Medicare Administrative Contractors (MACs) make regional coverage decisions
local_coverage_determination.variability_note: Coverage can vary significantly by MAC region

coverage_with_evidence_development.description: CMS may cover a treatment conditionally while requiring additional evidence collection
coverage_with_evidence_development.implications_for_protocol: Protocols should consider CED-ready endpoints and real-world data collection plans

key_coverage_criteria: Step therapy requirements (try cheaper alternatives first)
Real-world evidence requirements for continued coverage
preferred_evidence_types: Randomized controlled trials with active comparator

key_coverage_criteria: Zero-dollar copay programs for preferred drugs
Specialty pharmacy dispensing through CVS Specialty

Cigna_Express_Scripts.formulary_approach: Aggressive formulary management via Express Scripts
common_denial_triggers: Exceeds annual cost threshold without outcomes data

BlueCross_BlueShield.formulary_approach: Varies by state plan — generally conservative
key_coverage_criteria: Blue Distinction Centers for specialty care
common_denial_triggers: Not medically necessary per clinical policy bulletin
Alternative effective treatment available at lower cost

oncology.key_drivers: Companion diagnostic requirements, NCCN compendia listing, prior authorization complexity
oncology.mitigation: Include NCCN-recognized endpoints, biomarker-driven enrollment, comparative effectiveness vs SOC

cardiology.key_drivers: MACE endpoint requirements, CV outcomes trial expectations, step therapy policies
cardiology.mitigation: Include MACE composite endpoint, CV outcomes data, heart failure hospitalization endpoints

neurology_alzheimers.key_drivers: Surrogate endpoint debates (amyloid PET), clinical meaningfulness of CDR-SB changes, high drug costs
neurology_alzheimers.mitigation: Include clinically meaningful cognitive endpoints, caregiver burden measures, functional assessments

rare_disease.key_drivers: Small sample sizes, lack of natural history data, ultra-high drug pricing
rare_disease.mitigation: Natural history comparator, patient preference data, long-term follow-up commitments

immunology.key_drivers: Biosimilar competition, step therapy requirements, head-to-head data expectations
immunology.mitigation: Head-to-head vs biosimilar, disease activity scores, PRO endpoints (HAQ-DI, DLQI)

diabetes_metabolic.key_drivers: Crowded market, CV safety requirements, step therapy with metformin/sulfonylurea
diabetes_metabolic.mitigation: CV outcomes trial, HbA1c + body weight endpoints, hypoglycemia rates, patient convenience data

amcp_dossier_requirements.description: AMCP Format for Formulary Submissions — standard format for payer evidence dossiers in the US
required_sections: Economic Value and Outcomes (cost-effectiveness, budget impact)
critical_evidence_elements: Head-to-head comparisons with current standard of care
Budget impact model for a typical health plan
Subgroup analyses for key payer populations
Real-world evidence plan or existing RWE data

icer_value_framework.description: Institute for Clinical and Economic Review — influential US health technology assessment body
icer_value_framework.impact: ICER reviews influence PBM formulary decisions and state Medicaid coverage
protocol_implications: Include quality of life endpoints (EQ-5D-5L preferred)
Collect resource utilization data for economic modeling
Plan for long-term follow-up to demonstrate durability of effect
Include active comparator arm with current standard of care
